seo.title: AP Psych Score Distribution 2025 | Pass Rate and 5 Rate Explained
seo.description: Explore the AP Psych score distribution, pass rate, and 5 rate, and learn what the results may mean for your estimated AP Psychology score.

content.directAnswer: AP Psych score distribution pages help students understand how many test takers receive each score, but distribution data should be interpreted separately from your personal score estimate.

sections.heading: How to Read AP Psych Score Distribution
sections.body: Score distribution shows the share of students receiving each AP score from 1 to 5. It does not directly reveal the raw-score cutoff for your own exam.

sections.heading: Pass Rate and 5 Rate
sections.body: The pass rate usually refers to the share of students earning 3 or higher, while the 5 rate refers to the share earning the top score.

faqs.question: Is this calculator official?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator is an independent estimate and is not affiliated with or endorsed by College Board.

faqs.question: Can actual AP score cutoffs vary?
faqs.answer: Yes. Actual score thresholds can vary by year and exam form, so this tool should be used for planning only.

faqs.question: Does FRQ performance matter for AP Psych?
faqs.answer: Yes. FRQ performance contributes to the composite estimate, so a stronger FRQ score can offset some missed multiple-choice questions.

faqs.question: Should I use this before or after the exam?
faqs.answer: You can use it before the exam for target planning or after the exam to estimate possible score outcomes.
